circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: Yas Marina Circuit, a 5. 281 km track on Abu Dhabi's Yas Island designed by Hermann Tilke, has been the venue for Formula One's season finale since 2009. The circuit represents the second Middle Eastern Formula One destination, following Bahrain and preceding venues in Qatar and Saudi Arabia.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: A street circuit encircling Albert Park Lake in Melbourne's Albert Park suburb, this venue hosts the Australian Grand Prix and other racing events annually while maintaining FIA Grade 1 certification. The track serves as the premier racing destination for multiple motorsport categories, including the Supercars Championship Melbourne round and its supporting race program.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: Located in Spielberg, Austria, this 4. 326 km circuit was originally established as the Österreichring and held the Austrian Grand Prix annually from 1970 to 1987. Following a period of reconstruction and redesign, the track was reopened as the A1-Ring and returned to hosting the Austrian Grand Prix between 1997 and 2003.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: The Baku City Circuit is a street circuit in Azerbaijan's capital, positioned along Baku Boulevard, with a lap distance of 6. 003 km that ranks as the fourth-longest track in Formula One. The venue first hosted a grand prix in 2016 as the European Grand Prix, before becoming the permanent home of the Azerbaijan Grand Prix from 2017 onwards.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: Opened in 2004, the 5. 412 km Bahrain International Circuit hosts the Formula One Bahrain Grand Prix, marking the Middle East's inaugural F1 race. Beyond Formula One, the FIA Grade 1-licensed facility accommodates various motorsport categories including GP2 Series, drag racing, and 24-hour endurance competitions, with multiple circuit configurations available.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, the Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya spans 4. 657 kilometers and features a balanced design combining extended straights with diverse corner configurations, making it suitable for testing multiple racing disciplines. The venue holds an FIA Grade 1 license and accommodates up to 140,700 spectators across its facilities.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: Situated in the Walloon region of Belgium near the town of Spa, this 7-kilometre circuit has been a fixture of Formula One since the sport's inaugural 1950 season. The venue has continuously hosted the Belgian Grand Prix annually from 1985 onwards, with only two exceptions in 2003 and 2006, making it one of the championship's most enduring racing destinations.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: Located on Montreal's Notre Dame Island, this 4. 361-kilometre circuit hosts the FIA Formula One Canadian Grand Prix annually. Beyond Formula One, the track has accommodated various international racing series including world sportscar championships, open-wheel competitions, and multiple North American racing categories throughout its history.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: Located in Shanghai's Jiading District, the Shanghai International Circuit serves as China's premier motorsport venue, most notably hosting the Chinese Grand Prix during 2004-2019 and resuming from 2024. The facility has expanded its racing calendar to include the Shanghai ePrix from 2024 onwards.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Silverstone is an English racing venue located near Northamptonshire that has been the traditional home of the British Grand Prix since 1948 and notably hosted the inaugural World Championship race in 1950. After the Grand Prix alternated among three circuits between 1955 and 1986, Silverstone became its permanent base from 1987 onwards and continues to stage motorcycle racing events.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: The Hungaroring near Budapest became the first Formula One venue within the Eastern Bloc when it hosted its inaugural Grand Prix in 1986, a historic breakthrough following Bernie Ecclestone's pursuit of a Soviet race. This 4. 381 km circuit, completed in just eight months near a major highway outside the city, was built faster than any other Formula One track at the time.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: Located near Milan in northern Italy, the Autodromo Nazionale Monza is a 5. 793 km circuit constructed in 1922 as Europe's oldest purpose-built racing venue. The track has continuously hosted the Italian Grand Prix since 1949, earning the nickname "Temple of Speed" for its characteristically long straights and fast-cornering design.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: Suzuka Circuit is a 5. 807 km motorsport venue in Mie Prefecture, Japan, operated by Honda Mobilityland with a capacity of 155,000 spectators. The track is renowned primarily for hosting Formula One races and Japanese Super Formula competitions.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a street course that navigates through Paradise, Nevada, running alongside the famous Las Vegas Boulevard and its collection of luxury hotels and casinos. The layout incorporates several iconic Las Vegas landmarks, weaving between major destinations like MSG Sphere, Caesars Palace, Bellagio, and Paris Las Vegas as competitors traverse the urban track.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: This 4. 3-kilometer Mexico City circuit was renamed in 1979 to honor racing brothers Ricardo and Pedro Rodríguez, both of whom died in racing accidents. The track has hosted Formula One's Mexican Grand Prix across multiple eras, most recently on a continuous basis since 2015 after a 23-year hiatus, now branded as the Mexico City Grand Prix.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: Miami International Autodrome is a temporary street circuit constructed around Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens that measures 3. 363 miles with 19 corners and generates average qualifying speeds near 139 mph. Developed by specialist circuit designers Apex Circuit Design, the venue was purpose-built to host the Miami Grand Prix when it joined the Formula One calendar in 2022.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The Circuit de Monaco winds through the streets of Monte Carlo and La Condamine in the Principality of Monaco, stretching 3. 337 kilometers around the harbor. Though officially named after the circuit itself, it is widely known as the Monte Carlo circuit due to its location predominantly within that neighborhood.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: Located in the Dutch coastal dunes near Amsterdam, Circuit Zandvoort is a 4. 259 km track that restored Formula One racing to the Netherlands beginning in 2021 with the Dutch Grand Prix. The venue will host the event until 2026, when its Formula One commitment concludes.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: The Lusail International Circuit sits north of Doha in Qatar and spans 5. 419 kilometers with a 1. 068-kilometer main straight.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: São Paulo's Interlagos circuit, inaugurated in 1940, spans 4. 309 kilometers and has hosted Formula One racing for decades. Renamed in 1985 to commemorate the late driver José Carlos Pace, who perished in an aviation accident in 1977, the track operates in a counterclockwise direction and features a karting facility dedicated to fellow Brazilian racing legend Ayrton Senna.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: Located along Jeddah's waterfront on the Red Sea, the Jeddah Corniche Circuit is a street-based racing venue that has served as the home of the Saudi Arabian Grand Prix in Formula One since its debut in 2021, while also hosting international motorsport events.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: The Marina Bay Street Circuit winds through the Downtown Core and Kallang districts of Singapore, utilizing public roads that encircle the Marina Bay waterfront area.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, the 4. 657 km circuit features a balanced combination of extended straights and diverse corner sequences that test multiple aspects of driver and car performance. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 status and can accommodate approximately 140,700 spectators across its facilities.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: The Circuit of the Americas in Austin, Texas is a 3. 426-mile Grade 1 FIA track that hosts the Formula One United States Grand Prix alongside major motorsport events including MotoGP and endurance racing competitions. Beyond its primary role in international racing, the venue has accommodated numerous series throughout its history, from IndyCar to various sports car championships.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Alexander Albon is a Thai and British motorsport competitor who races for Williams in Formula One, representing Thailand.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: Spanish driver Fernando Alonso competes for Aston Martin in Formula One, where he has secured two world championships with Renault in 2005 and 2006 while accumulating 32 Grand Prix victories over 23 seasons. Beyond single-seaters, Alonso has achieved remarkable success in endurance racing, winning the 2018-19 World Endurance Championship and two Le Mans 24 Hours titles with Toyota, making him the sole driver to have claimed both a Formula One world title and a world sportscar championship.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Italian driver Andrea Kimi Antonelli represents Mercedes on the Formula One grid, having claimed five race victories following his entry into the sport in 2025.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: British driver Oliver James Bearman currently races in Formula One, competing for the Haas team.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Gabi Bortoleto is a Brazilian driver currently racing in Formula One with Audi's team.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: Valtteri Bottas, a Finnish motorsport competitor, currently drives for Cadillac in Formula One. The veteran driver has achieved ten Grand Prix victories throughout his 13-season career and twice secured second place in the championship standings while driving for Mercedes during 2019 and 2020.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Franco Alejandro Colapinto, an Argentine motorsport competitor, currently drives for Alpine in Formula One racing.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Jack Doohan is an Australian driver who holds a reserve role at Haas while competing in European Le Mans Series endurance racing. He made seven Formula One race starts between 2024 and 2025.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: Pierre Gasly is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Alpine. The 2020 Italian Grand Prix victory remains a significant achievement from his time with AlphaTauri.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Isack Alexandre Hadjar represents France in Formula One competition while driving for Red Bull Racing, though he holds dual French and Algerian nationality.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: Lewis Hamilton is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Ferrari who has achieved a record-tying seven World Drivers' Championships alongside Michael Schumacher. He holds multiple circuit records including the most race victories, pole positions, and podium finishes in the sport's history.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: German driver Nicolas Hülkenberg currently races in Formula One for Audi, while his career has also included significant success in endurance motorsport, notably winning the prestigious 24 Hours of Le Mans in 2015 driving for Porsche.

driver:LAW.title: Liam Lawson
driver:LAW.summary: Liam Lawson, a New Zealand racing driver, currently competes in Formula One representing Racing Bulls.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: Charles Leclerc represents Monaco in Formula One competition for Ferrari, where he has secured eight race victories throughout his nine-season tenure in the sport. The talented driver finished second in the 2022 World Drivers' Championship while driving for the Maranello-based team.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Danish driver Kevin Magnussen split his Formula One career across two separate stints, competing from 2014-2020 and again from 2022-2024. Currently, he races in the World Endurance Championship as a factory BMW driver for WRT while maintaining a part-time NASCAR Cup Series presence with Trackhouse Racing.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: Lando Norris, a British driver, secured the 2025 Formula One World Drivers' Championship while competing for McLaren. Across his eight seasons in the sport, he has claimed victory in 11 Grand Prix races.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: Esteban Ocon, a French racing driver, currently competes in Formula One for Haas. Earlier in his career, he achieved his sole Grand Prix victory at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix while driving for Alpine.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: Sergio Pérez is a Mexican motorsport competitor currently driving for Cadillac in Formula One. The veteran driver finished second in the 2023 drivers' championship while with Red Bull and has achieved six race victories throughout his 15 seasons in the sport.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Oscar Piastri, an Australian driver for McLaren, has claimed nine Formula One victories during his four-season career in the sport.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: Daniel Ricciardo, an Australian driver known as "the Honey Badger," competed in Formula One over 14 seasons between 2011 and 2024, securing eight race victories during his career in the sport.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: George Russell, a British driver competing for Mercedes, has secured seven Grand Prix victories during his eight-year Formula One career.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: Spanish driver Carlos Sainz competes for Williams in Formula One, where he has achieved four Grand Prix victories throughout his 12-season career in the sport.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: Logan Sargeant is an American driver who raced in Formula One during 2023 and 2024 before transitioning to other motorsport categories. He currently competes in the FIA World Endurance Championship's LMGT3 class representing Proton Competition, having previously driven in IMSA's LMP2 category.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Lance Stroll is a Canadian Formula One driver currently racing for Aston Martin in the sport's premier championship.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Yuki Tsunoda is a Japanese driver who has held reserve roles with Red Bull Racing and Racing Bulls throughout his Formula One involvement from 2021 to 2025.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: Dutch-Belgian driver Max Verstappen races for Red Bull Racing, where he has secured four consecutive world championships spanning 2021 through 2024. Over his 12-season career, he has accumulated 71 Grand Prix victories.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu holds the distinction of being the sole Chinese driver to have competed in Formula One, participating in the championship from 2022 through 2024. He currently functions as a reserve driver for the Cadillac team in the sport.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: Alpine Racing operates from its Enstone base as the successor to Renault's factory team, having been rebranded in 2021 to represent the French marque's Alpine sports car line before Renault's departure from the championship in 2025. The team's sole victory in its Alpine identity came through Esteban Ocon's triumph at Budapest in 2021, competing with Mercedes powerplants and holding a French racing licence.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: Aston Martin, a British automotive manufacturer, has maintained a presence in Formula One through different organizational structures, with the modern iteration operating as a competitive entry in the championship.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Scuderia Ferrari, the racing arm of the Italian luxury automaker, stands as Formula One's longest-running and most accomplished team, participating in every world championship since the sport's inaugural 1950 season. Known colloquially as "the Prancing Horse" after their iconic emblem, the Maranello-based outfit has established itself as the sport's most storied competitor.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: An American-licensed Formula One team founded by NASCAR owner Gene Haas in 2014, the outfit commenced racing in 2016 with a focus on competitive efficiency. Ayao Komatsu assumed the principal's role in early 2024, succeeding Guenther Steiner who had led the team since its establishment.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: A Swiss engineering and racing company founded in 1970, Sauber initially competed in endurance racing where it secured two world championships and won the 1989 Le Mans 24 Hours with Mercedes-Benz power. The team entered Formula One in 1993 as an independent constructor and competed through various iterations until 2025, becoming the fourth-longest-running constructor by race starts before Audi acquired its assets in 2024 to establish its own F1 programme.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren Racing is a British Formula One outfit headquartered in Woking that operates as a subsidiary within the McLaren Group, with ownership held by Mumtalakat Holding Company.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: Mercedes has maintained a continuous presence in Formula One since 1954 as both a constructor and power unit supplier, with its current Brackley-based squad operating under a German licence and competing as Mercedes-AMG Petronas. Since January 2022, ownership has been equally distributed among Mercedes-Benz Group, Ineos, and Toto Wolff. The team is traditionally known as the "Silver Arrows" within the sport.

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that commenced competition in 2024, operating as one of two Red Bull-owned squads alongside Red Bull Racing. Based in Faenza with additional facilities in Milton Keynes, the team functions as part of the Austrian conglomerate's dual-team Formula One strategy.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that began competing in 2024 as part of Red Bull GmbH's two-team operation. Based in Faenza with facilities near its sister team in Milton Keynes, the squad represents the Austrian conglomerate's second constructor entry alongside Red Bull Racing.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing, operating under an Austrian licence from its English base, emerged as a Formula One competitor in 2005 and became one of two world championship teams owned by the Red Bull conglomerate. Christian Horner led the organization from its inception through 2024, after which Laurent Mekies assumed the role of team principal.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams Grand Prix Engineering is a British Formula One constructor founded in 1977 by Frank Williams and Patrick Head, emerging from the ashes of Williams's previous failed racing venture. Headquartered at a Grove, Oxfordshire facility, the team competes in 2026 under the Atlassian Williams F1 Team designation.
